plain_language: Today: White House weighs an order to vet AI models before release. Cerebras launched a 26 billion dollar IPO. Critical bugs in n8n and Flowise let attackers take over AI workflows.
findings:
Today's report is anchored by the most consequential federal-AI-policy pivot of 2026: the May 4-5 New York Times reporting that the Trump administration is weighing an executive order creating a federal working group of tech executives + government officials to design a pre-release vetting process for new AI models, with senior administration officials briefing Anthropic, Google, and OpenAI on the plans last week. The immediate catalyst is Anthropic's Claude Mythos — announced Apr 7, with the model finding tens of thousands of zero-day vulnerabilities including a 27-year-old OpenBSD bug — and Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei publicly framing a cyber 'moment of danger.' Pairs with the May 1 UK AI Safety Institute cyber evaluation that placed GPT-5.5 at 71.4% and Mythos at 68.6% on Expert-tier offensive-cyber tasks (Opus 4.7 at 48.6%) and recorded GPT-5.5 + Mythos completing AISI's 32-step 'The Last Ones' corporate-network attack range end-to-end in 2 of 10 and 3 of 10 attempts respectively (an AISI-estimated 20-human-hour exploit chain), and the UK NHS ordering tech leaders to wall off hundreds of open-source GitHub repositories with a May deadline citing Mythos exposure. Together this is the cleanest week-over-week structural confirmation of the frontier cyber-AI export-control prediction (May 3) since the prediction was authored — the de-facto Project Glasswing posture is operating ahead of any formal BIS / Commerce category, and the EO route now reframes the agentic-AI security landscape as a regulator-policy story rather than a vendor-stack story. Separately the autonomous-weapons-carve-out prediction (May 2) gains a moderate-strength substrate signal: capability-conditional access carve-outs are now operationally road-tested in cyber via Project Glasswing's vetted-partner routing, providing the contractual template a parallel weapons carve-out would extend.

The second structural surprise is the Cerebras Systems IPO launch on May 4 — pricing 28M shares at $115-$125 each to raise approximately $3.5B at a $26.6B market cap at the high end (vs Series H $23B Feb 2026), would be the largest tech IPO of 2026 if priced at the high end, $510M trailing revenue per S-1, OpenAI partnership disclosed at >$10B multi-year warrant + loan structure (Dec 2025 OpenAI loaned Cerebras $1B secured by warrants for >33M shares ~12% of the float), angel investor roster including Sam Altman + Greg Brockman + Ilya Sutskever + Adam D'Angelo. The $26.6B target sits squarely above the Cerebras IPO prediction (May 2)'s $22-25B band — the valuation half lands the prediction in 'landing' shape with the first-day pop magnitude as the only empirical leg outstanding once trading opens. The IPO surfaces the first public-market comparable for AI-accelerator-startup equity and reframes the Groq / SambaNova / Tenstorrent cohort as the next Pareto-IPO candidates; the today-authored AI-accelerator IPO cohort prediction (May 6, not validated against itself) extends this thesis to a Q4 2026 second deal.

The third structural surprise is the no-code AI-workflow-harness CVE wave landing in the same window: n8n CVE-2026-21858 (CVSS 10.0 unauthenticated RCE via webhook content-type confusion, codenamed Ni8mare by Cyera Research Labs, patched in 1.121.0, with downstream prompt-injection scenarios via the n8n LLM-chatbot node; Canadian Centre for Cyber Security + Australian ASD ACSC + Censys + Orca Security + CSO Online publish coordinated advisories), Flowise CVE-2026-41264 (prompt-injection-driven RCE on the CSV Agent node where a chatflow user coaxes the LLM into emitting attacker-controlled Python the Flowise server executes), LMDeploy CVE-2026-33626 SSRF (first exploited within 12 hours 31 minutes against Sysdig Threat Research honeypots), Georgia Tech's Vibe Security Radar logging 35 CVEs in March 2026 directly to AI coding tools with researchers estimating 5-10x undercount across the open-source ecosystem. This wave delivers maximum-strength confirmation on the agentic-AI CVE-as-CSAI-CNA-sub-corpus prediction (May 1), strong-strength confirmation on the agent-skills attack-surface MITRE-style threat-sub-matrix prediction (Apr 29) and the agent-skills threat-sub-matrix MITRE/NIST extension prediction (Apr 30), moderate-strength confirmation on the LLM-tooling-Linux-kernel CVE chain prediction (May 2), and revives the dormant indirect-prompt-injection-as-top-CVE-category prediction (Apr 19) at strong strength via the Flowise textbook prompt-injection-driven RCE plus the n8n downstream prompt-injection file-leak path. Today's news section also names a today-authored CISA-publishes-agentic-AI-workflow-platform-secure-deployment-guide prediction (May 6, not validated against itself) explicitly anchored on this CVE wave.

Mistral AI Workflows public preview lands as the EU-frontier-lab counter-shape: Temporal-powered orchestration engine for enterprise multi-step AI processes, Python-defined workflows composing models + agents + external connectors with durability + observability + fault tolerance, control plane on Mistral-managed infrastructure with execution workers + data inside the customer environment, already in production at ASML + ABANCA + CMA-CGM + France Travail + La Banque Postale + Moeve. Workflows is the middle layer of Mistral's three-part stack (Forge for custom-model training March 2026 GTC launch at the bottom, Workflows in the middle, Vibe coding-agent platform at the top). This advances the Microsoft-Agent-365-MCP-policy prediction (May 2) and the cross-cloud agent-identity + MCP registry RFC prediction (May 3) at moderate strength via cross-vendor parity at the customer-control-plane layer, and the multi-cloud frontier-model SLA standardization prediction (May 1) at light strength via the OpenAI-on-AWS-Bedrock + Mistral-EU-orchestration plurality.

The AMD Q1 2026 print carries forward into the day-after window with HSBC's May 4 downgrade to Hold on +77% rally adding the first sell-side caution layer: Q1 revenue $10.25B (+38% YoY), Data Center $5.78B (+57% YoY) bundled EPYC + Instinct, Q2 2026 guide $11.2B (+/- $300M) vs Street $10.52B (+50.6% YoY at midpoint), Lisa Su declares Data Center the primary growth driver. The AMD AI-accelerator-revenue 10-Q footnote prediction (May 4) and the foundation-lab-audited-revenue prediction (Apr 30) carry at moderate-light strength — the disclosure-pressure thesis is now active for the Q2 10-Q filing window but no actual footnote ships, and Anthropic's >$30B run-rate continues to publish via journalist channels rather than audited cadence.

The production-serving Pareto split deepens with SGLang's Jan 2026 RadixArk spinout at ~$400M (Accel-led round), Cloudflare Infire's edge-distributed prefill/decode disaggregation, vLLM's batch / multi-hardware footprint, and SGLang's ~16,200 tok/s vs vLLM ~12,500 tok/s 29% throughput gap on shared-context workloads. The KV-cache-compression dtype prediction (Apr 29) and the local-LLM-stacks unified compressed-KV-cache flag prediction (Apr 30) carry at light strength: the substrate hardens but no fresh dtype CLI flag ships. The dormant GGUF-supply-chain-signed-cards prediction (Apr 21) revives at moderate strength via the Unsloth Mistral-coordinated GGUF fix loop plus the standing SGLang jinja2 SSTI baseline plus the new workflow-platform CVE wave — chat_template signing + pre-upload SSTI scanning continues to sharpen as a missing supply-chain control across Hugging Face / Ollama / ModelScope / Foundry Local / Bedrock.

Secondary signals: Boston Dynamics Atlas commercial production + Tesla Optimus Gen 3 ramp carry (the humanoid duopoly enters commercial production in a single quarter, Atlas $140K+ enterprise vs Optimus $20K-$30K consumer); the Andon Labs Stockholm AI-run cafe (LLM operator named Mona ordered 120 eggs in a stoveless cafe, then suggested using a high-speed oven, then ordered 22.5 kg of canned tomatoes for fresh-tomato spoilage) extends the LLM-autonomy-in-real-economy operational-evidence cohort to a second public-facing operator-of-record; the arxiv reasoning + agent + multimodal wave (Thinking in Text and Images interleaved vision-language reasoning traces for long-horizon robot manipulation, RecursiveMAS recursive-multi-agent collaborative reasoning, AutoResearchBench scientific-literature discovery, Small Language Models are the Future of Agentic AI) compounds the academic supply-chain layer of the user's third standing prediction.
relation_to_my_preds:
The user's first standing prediction (malicious local LLM → malware + zero-trust as fundamental safeguard) gains its strongest single-week reinforcement on both the supply-chain attack surface and the regulator-policy frame. The n8n CVE-2026-21858 CVSS-10 Ni8mare unauthenticated RCE on a no-code AI-workflow harness widely deployed in Tier-2 enterprises, the Flowise CSV Agent CVE-2026-41264 prompt-injection-driven RCE where the LLM agent itself emits attacker-controlled Python that the host server executes, and the LMDeploy CVE-2026-33626 SSRF first-exploited within 12 hours 31 minutes against Sysdig Threat Research honeypots, all coordinated through Canadian Centre for Cyber Security + Australian ASD ACSC + Censys + Orca Security + CSO Online + GitLab Advisories within the same window, plus Georgia Tech's Vibe Security Radar 35-CVE March-2026 count, plus the standing SGLang CVE-2026-5760 jinja2 SSTI baseline (longest-running 2026 LLM-tooling CVE category, malicious-GGUF supply-chain vector still operative even after Apr 24 patch), confirm the prediction's threat-class at operational tempo: the agentic-AI tooling stack is now both first-target attack surface and the LLM-as-attacker-execution-vehicle in the same CVE wave. The White House EO trail forcing federal pre-release vetting of frontier models above a defined cyber-capability threshold, plus the AISI 32-step 'The Last Ones' corporate-network attack-range that GPT-5.5 + Mythos completed end-to-end against an AISI-estimated 20-human-hour exploit chain, plus the UK NHS GitHub-repository lockdown citing Mythos exposure, add the regulator-policy layer the prediction's zero-trust-as-fundamental-safeguard logic has been waiting for.

The user's second standing prediction (cloud APIs for advanced + local LLMs for daily, driven by SaaS price rises) gains a structural confirmation on both legs simultaneously. On the cloud-side, the Cerebras IPO launch at $26.6B target valuation surfaces the first public-market comparable for AI-accelerator-startup equity with $510M trailing revenue and the >$10B OpenAI multi-year warrant + loan structure on the public record — the cloud-flagship-API supply-contract path is now publicly priceable; AMD's Q2 2026 guide $11.2B against Street $10.52B with Lisa Su naming Data Center the primary growth driver and HSBC's May 4 downgrade to Hold on +77% rally bracket the cloud-flagship-API leg between organic ramp and stretched-valuation caution. On the local-side, Mistral AI Workflows public preview ships an EU-frontier-lab orchestration shape running execution workers + data inside the customer environment (cloud / on-prem / hybrid) with ASML + ABANCA + CMA-CGM + France Travail + La Banque Postale + Moeve already in production — the local + on-prem deployment path for production AI is operationalized at the orchestration layer. Cloudflare's Infire Rust inference engine entering global edge production with prefill/decode disaggregation joins the standing SGLang vs vLLM Pareto split (now commercially wrapped as RadixArk at ~$400M) — the production-serving substrate continues to converge toward the consumer-GPU + edge-distributed inference economics the prediction's local-LLM leg names. The Unsloth Mistral-coordinated 3.5 fix plus the standing 12x faster MoE training + ultra-long-context Reinforcement Learning + full fine-tunes export to GGUF + Qwen3.6-27B + Kimi K2.6 support carry the local-LLM training-stack Pareto-frontier forward week-over-week. The user's split (advanced cloud + daily local) remains economically viable on both sides this week.

The user's third standing prediction (RL/LLM-based forecasting performance improvement) gains a substrate-densification signal across deployment-substrate, academic-supply-chain, and real-world-deployment layers. On the substrate side, the SGLang RadixAttention prefix-cache that compounds on shared-context workloads (chatbots, RAG, multi-turn agentic) plus Cloudflare Infire's prefill/decode disaggregation are exactly the architectures long-horizon agentic reasoning workloads need at deployment scale — the inference substrate for RL-trained forecasting agents reasoning about their own context is converging on multiple production-serving frameworks at once. On the academic-supply-chain side, the May 5-6 arxiv wave shows multi-paper convergence within the same window: Thinking in Text and Images (interleaved vision-language reasoning traces for long-horizon robot manipulation), RecursiveMAS (multi-agent framework integrating recursive computation into multi-agent systems for collaborative reasoning via iterative latent-space interactions), AutoResearchBench (benchmarking AI agents on complex scientific-literature discovery), Small Language Models are the Future of Agentic AI (SLMs as the agentic-AI primitive), plus the standing Agentic Reasoning for Large Language Models corpus. On the real-world-deployment side, the Andon Labs Stockholm AI-run cafe (LLM operator Mona ordering 120 eggs without a stove, recommending a high-speed oven workaround, ordering 22.5 kg of canned tomatoes for fresh-tomato spoilage) provides the second public-facing operator-of-record in the autonomous-business cohort, with the failure mode catalogue now reproducible across geographies — the operational evidence that prediction-of-future-states reasoning has not yet generalized to real-world commercial decision-making is itself a signal feeding the user's prediction's exploration-vs-exploitation dynamics.
